channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: André de Paula assume Ministério da Agricultura na 4ª feira
item.description: André de Paula (PSD) will assume the Ministry of Agriculture on Wednesday, April 1st, 2026, replacing Carlos Fávaro, who is leaving the post to run for Senate re-election in Mato Grosso. The handover ceremony will take place at Embrapa's auditorium in Brasília at 3:00 PM. Rivetla Edipo, the executive secretary of Fisheries, will temporarily lead the Ministry of Fisheries and Aquaculture, as part of President Lula's strategy to keep various ministries under executive secretaries during the ministerial reform.

item.title: “Ninguém está autorizado a usar meu nome”, diz Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro declared that no one is authorized to use his name in public statements, following criticism for his speech at CPAC in the U.S., where he positioned Brazil as a supplier of critical minerals to the U.S., compared Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation to Donald Trump’s, and accused Lula of lobbying to prevent PCC and Comando Vermelho from being designated terrorist organizations. Government allies condemned his remarks as treasonous and strategically damaging to national sovereignty.

item.title: Caiado, sobre anistia: quando há proposta no plano de governo, passa a ser plebiscito
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated that a potential victory in the 2026 elections could be interpreted as a popular plebiscite on granting amnesty to those convicted for the January 8 events, including former President Jair Bolsonaro. He links the legitimacy of his potential election to public approval of such an amnesty measure.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro nega contato com Eduardo e diz que cumpre regras ‘de forma rigorosa’
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied any direct or indirect contact with the former president during his house arrest, stating he was unaware of a video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC in the U.S. Lawyers asserted Bolsonaro is strictly complying with imposed conditions, including bans on communication devices and video recording, and that the video was a third-party statement without his involvement. The defense submitted clarifications to the STF following a request by Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Ao contrário da esquerda, a extrema-direita se movimenta rapidamente — até no exterior
item.description: The far-right is mobilizing rapidly, even internationally, with coordinated actions at events like the summit with Latin American leaders, while the left remains slow and disorganized. The author criticizes historical allies of the far-right, such as the military and Zionists, pointing to a pattern of repression and alliances with foreign powers reminiscent of the 1964 coup. Despite the grim outlook, he places hope in the Brazilian people, who resist and rise even without being called to politics.

item.title: Análise: 1º discurso de Caiado abre fosso do pré-candidato do PSD com Flávio Bolsonaro e o bolsonarismo
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's first speech as PSD's pre-candidate for president signals a clear break from Flávio Bolsonaro and bolsonarism, positioning himself outside the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization. He criticizes polarization, advocates for moderation, science, and effective governance, arguing that the right must replicate successful state-level management models (e.g., Goiás, São Paulo) rather than rely on political inheritance. Caiado also implies that the Bolsonaro government failed to consolidate power, and that the PT only loses relevance when the right governs well.

item.title: Pré-candidato do PSD, Caiado acena para o agro e critica políticas do PT
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, PSD's pre-candidate for president, emphasized his long-standing defense of agribusiness since 1986, criticizing PT's interventionist policies and advocating for free markets and technological innovation as drivers of national development. He pledged to scale Goiás' agro-modernization policies—including tech investment and rare earth exploration—to the national level.

item.title: Lula faz crítica indireta a PowerPoint da “GloboNews” sobre Banco Master
item.description: President Lula indirectly criticized GloboNews for displaying an incomplete diagram about the Banco Master case, stating that the media will not show all the actions of his government. He noted that the presentation, which listed officials allegedly linked to Banco Master founder Daniel Vorcaro, was deemed inadequate and prompted GloboNews to issue a public apology, acknowledging the material was wrong and incomplete.

item.title: Análise: Caiado se lança de olho no eleitor de direita não bolsonarista e critica Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado launched his presidential pre-candidacy for the PSD, positioning himself as a right-wing alternative for conservative voters dissatisfied with radicalized bolsonarism. He directly attacked Flávio Bolsonaro with a medical analogy questioning his management capacity and defended broad amnesty to consolidate conservative support. Caiado stated that defeating Lula at the polls is 'easy,' but the real challenge is governing in a way that makes the PT irrelevant in the long term.

item.title: Caiado diz que usará ‘prerrogativas’ para anistiar Bolsonaro mesmo com posição contrária do STF
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated he will use his 'prerogatives' to grant amnesty to Jair Bolsonaro, even if the Supreme Federal Court (STF) opposes it, claiming the measure reflects the will of the majority. He said amnesty would be one of his first actions if elected and that he will not relinquish his constitutional powers, even if they conflict with the Judiciary. Caiado was officially selected by the PSD as its presidential candidate following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal.

item.title: Otoni se filia ao PSD e diz que vai ajudar Caiado chegar aos evangélicos
item.description: Federal deputy and pastor Otoni de Paula left the MDB and joined the PSD on Monday (30), with the mission of helping presidential candidate Ronaldo Caiado connect with the evangelical community. The party switch is part of a strategy to broaden religious support in Caiado's 2026 election campaign.

item.title: Lula anuncia Leonardo Barchini como novo ministro da Educação
item.description: President Lula announced Leonardo Barchini, the current executive secretary of the Ministry of Education, as the new education minister, replacing Camilo Santana, who will leave the post to run in the October elections. The appointment reflects Lula’s strategy of ensuring continuity by promoting experienced internal officials. During the event, the government delivered 107 educational works across 16 states, funded by R$ 413.49 million from the Novo PAC and MEC.

item.title: Bolsonaro não teve acesso a vídeo de Eduardo, diz defesa do ex-presidente a Moraes
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro told Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes that Bolsonaro had no access to or participation in the video recorded by his son, former federal deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, in the United States. The statement was made in response to a STF order requiring clarification on Eduardo’s claims about the video. The defense denied any direct involvement by the former president in the production or dissemination of the material.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado descarta compor com Flávio Bolsonaro e diz que PSD garantiu candidatura
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado ruled out forming a ticket with Flávio Bolsonaro, stating his move to the PSD was conditioned on the party guaranteeing him a presidential candidacy. He emphasized he is not seeking any other office, such as the Senate, and switched parties solely to viable his presidential run. The comments come amid speculation about right-wing realignments, including the possible Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema joining the PSD as a running mate for Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula anuncia novo ministro da Educação e rebate críticos: 'Vamos construir nossas próprias notícias'
item.description: Lula announced that Leonardo Barchini will become the new Minister of Education, replacing Camilo Santana, who is stepping down to support Elmano de Freitas' re-election campaign in Ceará. During a ceremony inaugurating 107 education projects nationwide, Lula urged government workers to spread the administration's achievements, declaring, 'we will build our own news' to counter misinformation. Barchini, a trusted aide of Santana and close to Haddad and Padilha, previously served as Haddad’s chief of staff in São Paulo and held roles in the MEC during Lula’s and Dilma’s administrations.

item.title: Com a escolha de Caiado na corrida à Presidência, qual será o papel de Eduardo Leite nas eleições?
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul and former PSD presidential pre-candidate for 2026, was sidelined after the party chose Ronaldo Caiado as its nominee to face Gilberto Kassab. Leite avoided directly commenting on Caiado, stated his role remains to be defined by the group, and affirmed he will remain governor until 2027, ruling out a Senate bid.

item.title: Convocado a decidir politicamente, STF prepara eleição-tampão direta no Rio
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Federal Court (STF) is being called upon to make a political decision regarding the supplementary election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, following Cláudio Castro’s resignation to avoid electoral impeachment. The goal is to prevent Douglas Ruas, a Bolsonarist-right candidate aligned with Castro, from assuming office until October, thereby favoring Eduardo Paes, who would benefit from a direct election scheduled for June. The article frames this as a partisan legal maneuver to cleanse the state of a 'criminal command' and restore democratic order.

item.title: O xerife e o herdeiro: Caiado na corrida eleitoral põe 2x1 da direita contra Lula
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's PSD presidential pre-candidacy creates a 2x1 dynamic on the right, positioning him as a pragmatic, institutional alternative alongside Flávio Bolsonaro, who mobilizes the bolsonarist base. Despite low first-round voting intention (3.6%), Caiado’s low rejection and strong governance image in public safety make him a potential aggregator of center-right votes, especially among the middle class and productive sectors. His 'Goiás model' contrasts with Flávio’s visceral style, but PT strategists bet on right-wing fragmentation to benefit Lula.

item.title: Após defender fim da polarização, Caiado propõe anistia ampla e irrestrita
item.description: Goias Governor and PSD presidential pre-candidate Ronaldo Caiado proposed a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty for those involved in the January 8 attacks and former President Jair Bolsonaro as his first act in office, aiming to pacify Brazil and end the cycle of political polarization. He argued that his position outside the current political poles qualifies him to lead this process, citing his consistent trajectory and vowing to scale up Goiás’ policies on rare earths, technology, public security, and results-based management to the national level.

item.title: Motta destaca avanços na educação e reforça papel da Câmara em projetos do setor
item.description: Chamber President Hugo Motta (Republicans-PB) highlighted education advancements during an event with President Lula, including the delivery of 107 infrastructure projects and quality internet access in 99,000 public schools. He emphasized the role of teachers, citing the approval of the National Teacher’s Card, National Teacher Induction Policy, the new National Education Plan, and the National Education System. Motta also noted reforms in federal university rector elections, the creation of the Federal Institute of the Paraibano Sertão, and pension contributions for graduate fellows to strengthen science and opportunity in Brazil.

item.title: Governo vai anunciar pacote contra alta do querosene de aviação com crédito emergencial para aéreas
item.description: The Lula government is preparing to announce emergency measures to mitigate the impact of rising aviation kerosene prices, driven by international oil price volatility due to Middle East conflicts. The package will include emergency credit for Brazilian airlines.

item.title: PF exonera agente que planejou matar Lula e Moraes em trama golpista
item.description: The Federal Police dismissed agent Wladimir Matos Soares, sentenced to 21 years for participating in the coup plot that planned to assassinate Lula, Alckmin, and Alexandre de Moraes. He had leaked classified information about Lula’s security to Bolsonaro allies and made death threats against STF ministers, including stating Moraes 'should have had his head cut off'. The dismissal follows the final judgment of the convictions of Nucleus 3 members in the coup attempt.

item.title: De olho em reeleição, Tarcísio anunciará expansão do metrô nesta terça
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas will announce on Tuesday, during the inauguration of Line 17-Gold of the metro, the expansion of the line to Paraisópolis, adding 4.6 km of tracks and four new stations to serve over 100,000 residents. The initiative aims to benefit communities such as Paraisópolis, Morumbi, and Vila Andrade, reinforcing his urban infrastructure agenda ahead of re-election. The symbolic signing will take place Tuesday, with the executive project tender to be published in the State Official Gazette.

item.title: Caiado lança pré-candidatura promete anistia a Bolsonaro e exalta experiência administrativa
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) announced his pre-candidacy for President of Brazil on September 30, promising amnesty for those convicted in the January 8 attacks, including former President Jair Bolsonaro and others accused of coup plotting. He emphasized his administrative experience as a key qualification to lead the nation.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro diz que desconhecia vídeo de Eduardo até ser intimada por Moraes
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro stated that they only became aware of the video recorded by Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC after being formally notified by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes. According to the defense, there was no prior knowledge of the recording made by Eduardo in the United States or its subsequent social media dissemination, and emphasized that the former president did not participate in the content. The video, recorded by Eduardo at the conservative conference, was allegedly intended to be shown to Bolsonaro, who is under house arrest and barred from accessing phones and social media.

item.title: Nikolas reage após Moraes multar caminhoneiros em R$ 7 bilhões
item.description: Federal Deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL) harshly criticized Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes after the imposition of R$7.1 billion in fines on truckers for blocking federal highways in 2022, calling him a 'coward' on social media. Ferreira contrasted Moraes’ handling of the trucker protests with his approach to cases like the Master Bank scandal and INSS fraud, implying bias in the minister’s rulings.

item.title: Nancy Pelosi rasgou discurso de Trump, e não documento de Lula
item.description: The viral video showing Nancy Pelosi tearing up a document allegedly from Lula is false; the footage actually dates from February 2020, when Pelosi ripped a copy of Trump's speech to Congress, calling it a 'manifesto of lies'. The caption linking the act to Lula and Brazil is misinformation. UOL Confere verified the claim and found no connection to the Brazilian president.

item.title: Fazenda propõe a bancos novo programa de renegociação de dívidas
item.description: The Ministry of Finance proposed a new debt renegotiation program to banks to alleviate household debt, which now consumes 29.3% of family income — a historical high. Unlike the 2023 Desenrola program, this initiative aims to include government incentives to improve terms for consumers, particularly for credit card and online shopping debt, while President Lula stressed that debts for asset-building (like real estate) are considered 'good'.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado diz que Flávio Bolsonaro ‘não tem vivência e experiência’ para ser presidente
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated that Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) 'lacks the experience and background to govern,' during the launch of his presidential pre-candidacy in São Paulo. Caiado suggested that Flávio's youth may lead to a lack of balance in governance. The comment comes amid the PSD's internal selection process, where Caiado was chosen as the party's presidential candidate following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: Caiado vai derrubar Flávio Bolsonaro?
item.description: This Monday's Papo Antagonista discussed Ronaldo Caiado's official presidential candidacy for the PSD, the low blows after the INSS CPMI ended, public clashes between Janja and Nikolas Ferreira, and rising antisemitism in Brazil. The title suggests Caiado may be positioned as a potential alternative to defeat Flávio Bolsonaro in 2026, though such a direct matchup remains uncertain.

item.title: Motta diz que Gleisi fez ‘grande trabalho’ e não faltou diálogo com governo apesar de ‘embates’
item.description: House Speaker Hugo Motta praised Minister Gleisi Hoffmann for her work in the Secretariat of Institutional Relations, emphasizing dialogue, respect, and commitment despite political tensions. He highlighted education as a priority of his leadership and thanked President Lula for establishing a federal institute in Patos (PB), his electoral stronghold, calling it the fulfillment of a 15-year dream.

item.title: Em resposta a Moraes, Bolsonaro diz desconhecer vídeo de Eduardo
item.description: Bolsonaro's defense denied he had prior knowledge of a video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC in the U.S., stating he only learned of it after being summoned by Justice Alexandre de Moraes. Bolsonaro is barred from accessing social media or communicating with the outside, and Moraes demanded clarification on whether the video was improperly transmitted. The ruling also reinforces drone flight bans and visitation limits during house arrest.

item.title: Girão cobra apuração de denúncias sobre uso de recursos públicos no Ceará
item.description: Senator Eduardo Girão (Novo-CE) called for an investigation into allegations published by Veja magazine regarding the alleged irregular use of public funds and possible electoral crimes in Ceará, involving parliamentary amendments with financial movements of up to R$ 800 million and potential impact on 50 municipal campaigns. He cited suspicions of organized crime, money laundering, and electoral offenses, stressing the need for rigorous, impartial investigations that respect due process. Girão compared the case to national operations like Operation Sem Desconto, stating that the misuse of parliamentary amendments is a systemic issue requiring urgent action to combat entrenched corruption in Brazilian politics and state structures.

item.title: Lula sanciona criação de Instituto federal em reduto eleitoral de Hugo Motta na Paraíba
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed into law the creation of the Federal Institute of the Paraíba Sertão in Patos (PB), the electoral stronghold of Chamber of Deputies President Hugo Motta. The law also restructures executive careers and creates over 17,000 positions in the Ministries of Education and Management and Innovation in Public Services, with an estimated budgetary impact of R$ 5.3 billion in 2026. Motta, present at the signing, thanked Lula, calling the institute's creation a 'dream' fulfilled after 15 years of waiting.

item.title: Ex-presidente do Pros questiona, no TSE, incorporação ao Solidariedade
item.description: Former Pros president Marcus Vinícius Chaves de Holanda challenged the party's incorporation into Solidariedade at the TSE, claiming documents were signed by Eurípedes Júnior, who lacked legitimacy after a February 2023 STF ruling. He sought to suspend the acts and annul the incorporation, but the rapporteur minister denied the injunction, referencing a prior similar rejection.

item.title: Defesa diz que Bolsonaro não teve acesso a vídeo de Eduardo nos EUA
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro told the STF that he had no knowledge or involvement in the video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro in the U.S., stating that Bolsonaro only learned of the case after being summoned. Lawyers argued there is no evidence of prohibited communication during his temporary humanitarian house arrest, as the video was made by a third party abroad. Justice Alexandre de Moraes had requested clarification due to the communication restrictions imposed on Bolsonaro.

item.title: A moto deixou de ser só solução barata e virou protagonista da mobilidade brasileira
item.description: By 2026, motorcycles in Brazil had evolved beyond being merely a low-cost transportation option to become a central component of urban mobility, work, and lifestyle. Their role expanded significantly as essential tools for commuting, income generation, and cultural expression, reflecting deeper structural shifts in transport and consumption patterns.

item.title: “Amigo não fala com outro”, diz Caiado sobre polarização
item.description: Former governor Ronaldo Caiado criticized Brazil's political polarization, stating that for the first time he saw parents stop speaking to their children and friends cease communication over ideological differences. He emphasized that, despite having won and lost elections before, the current level of division is unprecedented and undermines democratic discourse.

item.title: Defesa nega que Bolsonaro tenha tido acesso a vídeo de Eduardo nos EUA
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied that he had access to a video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro in the United States during CPAC, stating the content is merely a verbal statement by a third party without the former president's participation. Lawyers argued that Bolsonaro complies rigorously with the humanitarian house arrest measures imposed by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, and there is no evidence of prohibited communication. The video, in which Eduardo said he was recording it to show his father, prompted Moraes' request for clarification.

item.title: A explicação de Bolsonaro ao STF sobre vídeo de Eduardo
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied to the STF any current, direct or indirect communication with his son Eduardo Bolsonaro, after Minister Alexandre de Moraes requested explanations regarding a video mentioned by the former deputy. According to the lawyers, the content of the post refers only to a verbal statement by a third party during an event abroad, without the participation of the ex-president, and there is no evidence of the use of prohibited means under his temporary humanitarian house arrest.

item.title: Anistia a Bolsonaro, terras raras, combate ao crime: as propostas de Caiado no 1º discurso
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) of Goiás launched his pre-candidacy for the 2026 Brazilian presidency, proposing as his first act a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty for Jair Bolsonaro and others involved in institutional tensions, aiming to pacify the country. He also advocated for Brazil to lead in the exploration and processing of critical minerals like heavy rare earths through partnerships with the U.S. and Japan, and pledged to combat organized crime by integrating state and federal police forces.

item.title: PSD anuncia Caiado como pré-candidato à Presidência
item.description: The PSD announced Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as its pre-candidate for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior and the sidelining of Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite. Caiado will resign as governor on March 31, 2026, to focus on the campaign, with his vice-governor Daniel Vilela (MDB) succeeding him. In his announcement speech, Caiado denounced political polarization as an artificial construct benefiting those in power, asserting that Brazil can no longer endure the current climate.

item.title: Caiado vê vitória sobre Lula como provável, mas alerta para desafio de governar
item.description: Former Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado said defeating Lula in the 2026 elections would be easy, but warned that governing the country would be the real challenge. To counter polarization, he promised that, if elected, his first act would be to grant a general and unrestricted amnesty. He criticized the current administration and stressed the need to restore governability.

item.title: Caiado diz que anistiará Bolsonaro e outros golpistas se ganhar a eleição
item.description: Goiás governor and PSD presidential pre-candidate Ronaldo Caiado announced he would grant a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty to those involved in the January 8 coup attempt, including Jair Bolsonaro, as his first act if elected. He framed the measure as a way to pacify Brazil and overcome political polarization. The statement was made during the launch of his pre-candidacy in São Paulo, following Ratinho Jr.'s withdrawal and the party's decision led by Gilberto Kassab.

item.title: Crusoé: Caiado promete anistia para acabar com polarização. Vai colar?
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, former governor of Goiás, announced his presidential candidacy for 2026 under the PSD, positioning himself as a third way to counter the polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. He proposed amnesty as a key measure to reduce political conflict in Brazil. The strategy aims to attract voters dissatisfied with the two dominant ideological poles.

item.title: Eduardo e Michelle rebatem Moraes após pedido de explicações sobre vídeo
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro and former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro rebuked Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes after he demanded explanations regarding a video Eduardo claimed to have shown to Jair Bolsonaro, who is barred from using cell phones and social media. They accused Moraes of censorship and judicial persecution against the Bolsonaro family. The incident unfolds amid ongoing tensions between the former president’s allies and the STF.

item.title: Caiado lança pré-candidatura prometendo anistia a Bolsonaro e exaltando experiência administrativa
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), launched his presidential pre-candidacy promising a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty for those convicted in the January 8 attacks, including Jair Bolsonaro. He emphasized his administrative experience, public security, and stated his goal is to govern in a way that the PT is no longer a viable option. Caiado was chosen by the PSD after Ratinho Júnior withdrew, solidifying his position as the party's main candidate for 2026.

item.title: PL pede ao STF que próximo presidente da Alerj assuma interinamente o governo do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) asked the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to have the next president of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislative Assembly (Alerj) assume the governorship on an interim basis, challenging Minister Cristiano Zanin’s decision to appoint the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice as governor. The PL claims the decision is unconstitutional and that the TJ-RJ president should only act as a last-resort successor while the Alerj president is unable to serve. The petition follows the preventive detention of Rodrigo Bacellar (former Alerj president) and the conviction of Cláudio Castro for abuse of power in the 2022 elections.

item.title: Kassab decreta o fim da alternativa a Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, leader of the PSD, ended the expectation that his party could serve as a moderate alternative between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections. With Ratinho Junior’s definitive withdrawal from the race, the PSD’s project of institutional responsibility collapsed, reinforcing polarization. The article states that Kassab, as a national political strategist, no longer sees room for a viable third way.

item.title: Caiado promete anistia a Bolsonaro em 1º discurso após escolha do PSD
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás and PSD's presidential pre-candidate, advocated for 'deactivation' of political polarization in his first speech after the party's nomination, but promised a 'broad, general and unrestricted amnesty' for Jair Bolsonaro and others convicted in the 2022 coup attempt as his first government act. He positioned himself as an alternative to both PT and Bolsonarism, emphasizing his agribusiness background, technological innovation, and public security focus, stating that 'criminals are not made' in Goiás and that drug trafficking controls part of Brazil's territory.
